title: Unlock Opportunities with Bank Statement Loans
subtitle: Explore how Bank Statement Loans can empower self-employed borrowers by focusing on cash flow rather than traditional income documentation.
keyTakeaways: Identify clients with consistent bank deposits.
Structure loans using cash flow analysis.
Submit using PRO Portal for streamlined processing.
Build referral networks with local CPAs and business groups.

sections.heading: Spotting the Opportunity
sections.body: Identify clients with at least 12 months of consistent bank deposits who face challenges with traditional income documentation. Focus on small business owners, freelancers, and gig economy workers who might benefit from alternative loan structures.
bullets: Target clients with income documentation issues.
Look for consistent deposit patterns in bank statements.

sections.heading: Structuring the Loan
sections.body: Calculate income using deposits from bank statements, ensuring consistency with income trends. Consider non-traditional income sources like bonuses or irregular commissions as part of the cash flow analysis.
bullets: Use deposits, not withdrawals, for income calculation.
Include non-traditional income in cash flow analysis.

sections.heading: Documentation and Submission Tips
sections.body: Gather at least 12-24 months of personal or business bank statements. Include detailed income calculations and narrative explanations in your submission package. Use PRO Portal for secure document uploads and efficient pipeline management.
bullets: Upload documents securely in PRO Portal.
Ensure detailed income narrative is included.

sections.heading: Action Steps for Brokers
sections.body: Identify potential self-employed clients who struggle with traditional income documentation. Review recent bank statements to evaluate consistent cash flow. Discuss with your scenario desk about structuring these loans effectively.
bullets: Submit a sample case in PRO Portal.
Reach out to local CPAs for referrals.

metaDescription: Learn how to identify, structure, and submit Bank Statement Loans for self-employed borrowers using bank statements.
